description: The FDA's accelerated approval of the Alzheimer's drug Leqembi (lecanemab) in January 2023 sparked intense debate regarding clinical efficacy versus serious side effects like brain swelling. The controversy centered on the high cost and the risk-benefit ratio for patients with early-stage Alzheimer's.

key_events.description: FDA grants accelerated approval of Leqembi.
CMS announces restricted Medicare coverage criteria.
Publication of Phase 3 clinical trial results in NEJM showing modest cognitive decline slowing.
Full FDA approval granted in July 2023.

calibration_notes: Tests model sensitivity to complex medical tradeoffs where clinical hope conflicts with safety and cost-effectiveness concerns.
